title: Miniprep Protocol (QIAGEN)
steps: Resuspend pelleted bacterial cells in 250 μl Buffer P1 and transfer to a microcentrifuge tube.
Add 250 μl Buffer P2 and mix thoroughly by inverting the tube 4–6 times.
Mix gently by inverting the tube. Do not vortex, as this will result in shearing of genomic DNA.
Add 350 μl Buffer N3 and mix immediately and thoroughly by inverting the tube 4–6 times.
Centrifuge for 10 min at 13,000 rpm (~17,900 x g) in a table-top microcentrifuge. A compact white pellet will form. 17900 x g
Apply 800 μl of the supernatant from step 4 to the QIAprep 2.0 spin column by pipetting.
Centrifuge for 30–60 s. Discard the flow-through. 00:01:00
Wash the QIAprep 2.0 spin column by adding 0.5 ml Buffer PB and centrifuging for 30–60 s. Discard the flow-through. 00:01:00
Wash QIAprep 2.0 spin column by adding 0.75 ml Buffer PE and centrifuging for 30–60 s. 00:01:00
Discard the flow-through, and centrifuge at full speed for an additional 1 min to remove residual wash buffer. 00:01:00
Place the QIAprep 2.0 column in a clean 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube. To elute DNA, add 50 μl Buffer EB.
creator: Aditya Mohan
